Description: Verify user authentication with approved credentials
Execution steps: 1. Open the sign-in page. ; 2. Enter a valid approved username and password. ; 3. Select Sign In. ; 4. Verify access to the authorized landing page.

Description: Verify role-based access control for protected functions
Execution steps: 1. Sign in as a user without administrator privileges. ; 2. Attempt to open an administrator-only function. ; 3. Verify access is denied. ; 4. Sign in as an administrator. ; 5. Verify the function is accessible.

Description: Verify audit logging of sign-in attempts
Execution steps: 1. Attempt one successful sign-in and one failed sign-in. ; 2. Open the audit log as an authorized user. ; 3. Locate both events. ; 4. Verify each entry includes user, timestamp, and outcome.

Description: Verify account lockout after repeated failed sign-ins
Execution steps: 1. Use a valid user account. ; 2. Enter an incorrect password five times. ; 3. Attempt a sixth sign-in. ; 4. Verify the account is locked and a clear message is displayed.
1. Sign in as an administrator. ; 2. Select a test user account. ; 3. Initiate a password reset. ; 4. Verify the reset is recorded. ; 5. Verify the user can sign in using the temporary password.
1. Start a password change. ; 2. Enter a password that violates the configured policy. ; 3. Submit the change. ; 4. Verify the password is rejected with a validation message.

Description: Verify temporary password expiration after first use
Execution steps: 1. Reset a test user's password. ; 2. Sign in with the temporary password. ; 3. Complete the required password change. ; 4. Attempt to sign in again with the temporary password. ; 5. Verify it is rejected.

Description: Verify encryption of sensitive stored data
Execution steps: 1. Create a record containing approved test-sensitive data. ; 2. Inspect the stored database value using authorized access. ; 3. Verify the value is not stored in readable plaintext.

Description: Verify encryption of sensitive data in transit
Execution steps: 1. Start a browser session to the application. ; 2. Inspect the connection security details. ; 3. Submit a record containing test-sensitive data. ; 4. Verify transmission uses an approved encrypted protocol.

Description: Verify required-field validation on user records
Execution steps: 1. Open the new user form. ; 2. Leave a required field blank. ; 3. Submit the form. ; 4. Verify the record is not saved and the required field is identified.

Description: Verify clear validation messages for invalid input
Execution steps: 1. Enter an invalid value in a validated field. ; 2. Submit the form. ; 3. Verify an understandable validation message identifies the issue.
1. Create a user with a unique email address. ; 2. Attempt to create another user with the same email address. ; 3. Verify the second record is rejected.
1. Sign in as a test user. ; 2. Create a managed record. ; 3. View its audit metadata. ; 4. Verify creation time and creator are recorded.
1. Modify an existing managed record. ; 2. View its audit metadata. ; 3. Verify last modification time and modifier are updated.
1. Open the retention configuration as an administrator. ; 2. Review the audit-record retention setting. ; 3. Verify it is configured for at least seven years.

Description: Verify authorized record search by identifier
Execution steps: 1. Sign in as an authorized user. ; 2. Search for a known record identifier. ; 3. Verify the matching record is returned.

Description: Verify filtering by status and date range
Execution steps: 1. Open a record list. ; 2. Apply a status filter and a date-range filter. ; 3. Run the search. ; 4. Verify all returned records match both filters.
1. Search for a set of records that exceeds one page. ; 2. Navigate to the next page. ; 3. Verify additional results appear and page navigation is available.

Description: Verify export of authorized search results to CSV
Execution steps: 1. Run a record search as an authorized user. ; 2. Select Export. ; 3. Open the exported file. ; 4. Verify it is CSV format and contains the displayed authorized results.
1. Open an individual record. ; 2. Select Print or Generate Report. ; 3. Verify a printable report is generated with the record details.
1. Create two new records. ; 2. Record their generated identifiers. ; 3. Verify both identifiers are present and distinct.

Description: Verify deletion prevention for referenced records
Execution steps: 1. Identify a record referenced by an active transaction. ; 2. Attempt to delete it. ; 3. Verify deletion is prevented with an explanatory message.

Description: Verify authorized archival of inactive records
Execution steps: 1. Sign in as an authorized user. ; 2. Select an inactive record. ; 3. Archive the record. ; 4. Verify the record status changes to archived.

Description: Verify administrator-only restoration of archived records
Execution steps: 1. Sign in as a non-administrator and attempt to restore an archived record. ; 2. Verify access is denied. ; 3. Sign in as an administrator and restore it. ; 4. Verify restoration succeeds.
1. Open records in each available status. ; 2. Verify each displayed status matches a configured status value.
1. Assign a user while creating a record. ; 2. Save the record. ; 3. Verify the assigned user receives a creation notification.
1. Open an assigned record. ; 2. Reassign it to another user. ; 3. Save changes. ; 4. Verify the new assignee receives a notification.
1. Create a record with a due date within the notification threshold. ; 2. Run the notification process or wait for the scheduled job. ; 3. Verify the owner receives a due-date notification.
1. Open an unread notification. ; 2. Select Acknowledge. ; 3. Verify its status changes to acknowledged.
1. Trigger a notification. ; 2. Open notification history. ; 3. Verify recipient, timestamp, delivery outcome, and notification type are present.

Description: Verify synchronization with approved time source
Execution steps: 1. Open system time configuration or monitoring data. ; 2. Verify synchronization with the approved source is active. ; 3. Compare system time to the approved source within the allowed tolerance.
1. Create an auditable event. ; 2. Inspect its stored timestamp through authorized access. ; 3. Verify the stored value is in Coordinated Universal Time.
1. Set a user's preferred time zone. ; 2. Open a record with a known UTC timestamp. ; 3. Verify the displayed time is converted to the selected local time zone.
1. Use an approved load-test tool. ; 2. Simulate 200 active authenticated users. ; 3. Execute representative workflows. ; 4. Verify all sessions remain functional.
1. Under normal load, retrieve a representative record repeatedly. ; 2. Capture response times. ; 3. Verify standard retrieval operations complete within two seconds.
1. Under normal load, save representative records repeatedly. ; 2. Capture response times. ; 3. Verify standard save operations complete within three seconds.
1. Record the application availability time. ; 2. Perform an approved planned restart. ; 3. Measure the time until service is available. ; 4. Verify recovery is within fifteen minutes.
1. Review scheduled backup configuration and history. ; 2. Verify at least one successful backup exists for each 24-hour period.
1. Run or review a scheduled backup. ; 2. Review backup verification results. ; 3. Verify integrity validation completed successfully.
1. Select an approved backup. ; 2. Restore it to the designated recovery environment. ; 3. Verify the restored data is accessible and complete.
1. Stop or simulate loss of the application service in a test environment. ; 2. Observe monitoring alerts. ; 3. Verify an interruption alert is generated.
1. Configure or simulate remaining storage below 20 percent. ; 2. Run storage monitoring. ; 3. Verify a low-capacity alert is generated.
1. Sign in as a standard user. ; 2. Attempt each representative administrative function. ; 3. Verify all are inaccessible.
1. Sign in as a test user. ; 2. Leave the session inactive for 30 minutes. ; 3. Perform an action. ; 4. Verify reauthentication is required.

Description: Verify session identifier invalidation after sign-out
Execution steps: 1. Sign in and capture the session identifier through approved test tooling. ; 2. Sign out. ; 3. Attempt to reuse the identifier. ; 4. Verify access is denied.
1. Start an administrator sign-in. ; 2. Enter valid credentials. ; 3. Complete the configured second factor. ; 4. Verify access is granted only after successful multi-factor verification.

Description: Verify audit logging of security configuration changes
Execution steps: 1. Change a security-relevant configuration as an administrator. ; 2. Open the audit log. ; 3. Verify the change, actor, timestamp, and old/new values are recorded.
1. Open password policy settings as an administrator. ; 2. Configure a test expiration period. ; 3. Save settings. ; 4. Verify the setting persists and is applied to a test account.
1. Open notification preferences as an administrator. ; 2. Change a delivery preference. ; 3. Save and trigger a notification. ; 4. Verify delivery follows the selected preference.
1. Prepare an import file with an invalid field format. ; 2. Upload the file. ; 3. Verify validation identifies the invalid data and prevents invalid import.

Description: Verify rejection of unrecognized import columns
Execution steps: 1. Prepare an import file with an extra unsupported column. ; 2. Upload the file. ; 3. Verify the file is rejected and the unknown column is identified.

Description: Verify import error report for rejected rows
Execution steps: 1. Upload an import file containing invalid rows. ; 2. Open the import result. ; 3. Verify an error report identifies each rejected row and reason.

Description: Verify valid-row processing despite unrelated import errors
Execution steps: 1. Upload an import file containing both valid and invalid rows. ; 2. Complete the import. ; 3. Verify valid rows are processed and invalid rows are reported separately.
1. Upload an import file. ; 2. Open the import transaction result. ; 3. Verify it includes upload time, totals, successes, failures, and submitter.
1. Sign in as an authorized user. ; 2. Select Download Import Template. ; 3. Verify the downloaded template contains approved headers and guidance.
1. Send an authorized request to a published API endpoint. ; 2. Verify a successful response is returned with expected data.
1. Send a request with a valid approved API token. ; 2. Verify it succeeds. ; 3. Send the same request with an invalid token. ; 4. Verify it is rejected.
1. Send an invalid API request. ; 2. Inspect the response. ; 3. Verify it uses the documented error structure and status code.
1. Send requests exceeding the configured rate limit. ; 2. Verify excess requests receive the documented throttling response.
1. Call a supported published API version using a compatible request. ; 2. Verify the response remains compatible with the published contract.
1. Configure an outbound message with invalid required content. ; 2. Trigger transmission. ; 3. Verify transmission is blocked and a validation event is recorded.

Description: Verify retry of failed outbound integration
Execution steps: 1. Simulate a temporary external-system failure. ; 2. Trigger an outbound integration. ; 3. Restore the external service. ; 4. Verify retries occur according to policy and eventually succeed.
1. Simulate an integration failure. ; 2. Review integration logs. ; 3. Verify diagnostic details such as endpoint, time, correlation ID, and error are recorded.
1. Sign in as an authorized user. ; 2. Open integration status. ; 3. Verify current or recent integration states are displayed.
1. Sign in as a non-authorized user. ; 2. Attempt to edit a system configuration value. ; 3. Verify the edit is blocked.
1. Change a configuration value as an administrator. ; 2. Open configuration history. ; 3. Verify the actor, timestamp, old value, and new value are recorded.

Description: Verify approval requirement for production configuration changes
Execution steps: 1. Submit a production configuration change. ; 2. Attempt to apply it without approval. ; 3. Verify application is blocked. ; 4. Obtain approval and verify application succeeds.
1. Sign in as an administrator. ; 2. Open system information. ; 3. Verify the current application version is displayed.

Description: Verify online help for primary workflows
Execution steps: 1. Open each primary workflow. ; 2. Select the available help option. ; 3. Verify context-appropriate guidance is available.

Description: Verify accessible labels for interactive controls
Execution steps: 1. Open representative application pages using an accessibility inspection tool. ; 2. Inspect interactive controls. ; 3. Verify each control has an accessible label.
1. Open primary workflows. ; 2. Navigate using only keyboard controls. ; 3. Verify all required actions are reachable and usable.
1. Inspect representative pages using an approved contrast tool. ; 2. Verify text and interactive control contrast meets the applicable requirement.
1. Trigger a representative system error. ; 2. Review the user-facing message. ; 3. Verify it explains the issue without exposing sensitive technical details.
1. Start a destructive action such as deletion. ; 2. Verify a confirmation prompt appears. ; 3. Cancel the prompt and verify no change occurs. ; 4. Confirm the action and verify it proceeds.
1. Open an existing record and modify a field. ; 2. Select Cancel. ; 3. Reopen the record. ; 4. Verify the change was not saved.

Description: Verify preservation of user-entered data on validation failure
Execution steps: 1. Enter values in a form, including one invalid value. ; 2. Submit the form. ; 3. Verify validation occurs. ; 4. Verify valid entered values remain available for correction.
1. Open a supported list view. ; 2. Sort by each supported column. ; 3. Verify the displayed ordering changes correctly.
1. Open a supported list view. ; 2. Change visible-column settings. ; 3. Save preferences. ; 4. Verify the selected columns are displayed.
1. Set list-view preferences. ; 2. Sign out and sign back in. ; 3. Verify the preferences are retained.

Description: Verify unique external transaction correlation IDs
Execution steps: 1. Initiate two external transactions. ; 2. Inspect transaction records. ; 3. Verify each has a populated and unique correlation identifier.

Description: Verify correlation IDs in application logs
Execution steps: 1. Initiate an externally initiated transaction. ; 2. Search relevant application logs. ; 3. Verify the transaction correlation ID is present.

Description: Verify protection of log files from unauthorized modification
Execution steps: 1. Attempt to modify a log file using a non-authorized account. ; 2. Verify modification is denied.

Description: Verify log rotation according to retention policy
Execution steps: 1. Generate sufficient logs or advance a test environment to trigger rotation. ; 2. Inspect log storage. ; 3. Verify rotation and retention follow configured policy.
1. Call the health-check endpoint. ; 2. Verify it returns the expected success response when the application is healthy.
1. Make a dependent service unavailable in a test environment. ; 2. Call the health-check endpoint. ; 3. Verify dependency health is reported as degraded or unavailable.
1. Access a protected workflow using an unsupported browser version. ; 2. Verify access is blocked.
1. Access the application using an unsupported browser version. ; 2. Verify a message explains supported-browser requirements.

Description: Verify compatibility with approved operating systems
Execution steps: 1. Access representative workflows from each approved operating system. ; 2. Verify core workflows operate successfully.

Description: Verify upload type and size validation
Execution steps: 1. Attempt to upload a disallowed file type. ; 2. Verify rejection. ; 3. Attempt to upload an oversized allowed type. ; 4. Verify rejection with a clear message.
1. Upload an approved harmless test file that triggers the malware-scanning test response. ; 2. Verify the file is not made available. ; 3. Verify the event is recorded.
1. Upload a file with a known client filename. ; 2. Inspect stored file metadata through authorized access. ; 3. Verify the stored name is system-generated.
1. Attempt to download a protected uploaded file as an unauthorized user. ; 2. Verify access is denied. ; 3. Repeat as an authorized user and verify download succeeds.
1. Create a managed document. ; 2. Upload or save a revised version. ; 3. Open version history. ; 4. Verify both versions are listed.
1. Open a document with two versions. ; 2. Select Compare Versions. ; 3. Verify differences between selected versions are presented.

Description: Verify audit event for document downloads
Execution steps: 1. Download a managed document as an authorized user. ; 2. Open the audit log. ; 3. Verify a document-download event is recorded.
1. Open retention settings as an administrator. ; 2. Configure a retention period for a record category. ; 3. Save settings. ; 4. Verify the setting persists.

Description: Verify permanent deletion after retention expiry
Execution steps: 1. Create or identify a record past the approved retention period in a test environment. ; 2. Run the retention process. ; 3. Verify the record is permanently deleted.
1. Run the retention process. ; 2. Open the retention action report as an authorized administrator. ; 3. Verify completed retention actions are listed.

Description: Verify operational metrics for volume and response time
Execution steps: 1. Generate representative request activity. ; 2. Open operational metrics. ; 3. Verify request volume and response-time metrics are displayed.

Description: Verify administrator view of system error trends
Execution steps: 1. Generate or use recorded representative system errors. ; 2. Sign in as an administrator. ; 3. Open error-trend reporting. ; 4. Verify error trends are displayed.
